rationale: The citing paper uses Huang and Zhang (2011) in the core theory section to support a specific prior finding: at low progress, higher perceived velocity increases effort because attainability concerns are salient. This evidence is used to establish that prior goal-progress research gives competing predictions across progress stages, which directly motivates the paper's proposed moderator (evaluation mode). The citation is also revisited in the discussion to position the contribution within inference-based goal pursuit research, indicating a meaningful framing role rather than a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Huang and Zhang (2011) as part of the conceptual foundation showing stage-dependent motivation in goal pursuit. It builds from that literature to propose a different moderator, arguing that joint versus separate evaluation shifts donations toward charities farther from their goal.
